column1: This document explains how to interpret `current_candidate_ranking.csv` in the current version of **Programmable Neurorepair**.

column2: but it should no longer be read as the main output of the project or as a complete summary of what the system now is.

column1: It is best understood as an earlier and still informative layer inside a much broader architecture.

`current_candidate_ranking.csv` captures an earlier but important project layer: a structured ranking of candidate genes or control-relevant features associated with the repair-oriented side of the system.
At the time this ranking was most central
- which candidates most strongly align with repair-compatible logic
- which candidates appear repeatedly important across the project’s internal analysis
- which candidates may matter for later transition or control interpretation
This made the ranking a useful early synthesis layer.
The ranking mattered because it helped the project move beyond a purely descriptive single-cell readout.
- a way to distinguish stronger from weaker candidate relevance
- a bridge toward candidate control interpretation

column2: the project would have had a weaker basis for moving from raw state description toward more organized molecular interpretation.

column1: ## Why the ranking is no longer enough on its own
The project has now moved beyond a ranking-centric stage.
What now exists is broader than a candidate list. The project now includes:
That means `current_candidate_ranking.csv` should now be read as one layer inside a larger system
column2: not as the project’s final or most important output.

column1: ## How the ranking should be read now
The ranking is best understood as:
- a **historical bridge** between the earlier repair-signature phase and the later architecture phase
- **not** the full logic of the project
- the ranking helps show which candidates mattered
- the architecture helps show how some of those roles differ
- the stack and intervention layers help show how that logic becomes operational
This architecture should not be confused with a flat ranking.
- which candidates appear strong or recurrent
- what distinct role each candidate may be playing in the state-transition problem
The project is now stronger because it moved from:
The ranking also should not be confused with the repair signature.
The repair signature summarizes the molecular profile of the dominant repair-compatible state.
The ranking helps prioritize candidates associated with repair-oriented logic.
The ranking was an important input into the later control-layer reading
column2: but the current control-layer framework is more structured.

column1: This means the current system is no longer best read as “the top candidates are simply the most important in one generic sense.”
- or organize state quality in different ways
## Relation to stack and program logic
The ranking also now sits underneath later project layers.
It is no longer the operational top layer.
That means the ranking should now be read as an input into the broader logic
column2: not as the project’s main output.

column1: The project’s strongest current external support does not come from the ranking alone.
- Dataset B as the main first-pass external benchmark
- Dataset C as supportive mixed triangulation
This matters because the project is no longer only saying “these candidates rank highly.” It is also asking whether the broader architecture and state logic survive externally.
The ranking still supports the claim that:
- some candidates are more central than others
- the repair-oriented side of the system has a more structured molecular prioritization
- the project did not emerge from random marker choice
- the later architecture and control layers had a meaningful earlier prioritization base
## What this ranking does not support
The ranking does **not** support the claim that:
- ranking alone explains the whole project
- the top-ranked candidates alone are sufficient to define control logic
- a flat ranking is equivalent to a minimal architecture
- the ranking alone proves mechanism or therapeutic relevance
- the ranking is the most mature current reading of the project

column2: but the project has now moved beyond a ranking-centric stage into a broader architecture
